title: Time & Schedule on Site
goal: Talk about work times, breaks, and the daily schedule.

completionCriteria.summary: Finish the quiz with at least 80%.

vocabulary.example: We have a break at noon.
Do not be late for work.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: What time does your shift start?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: My shift starts at seven o'clock.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: Please do not be late tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: I will come early. When is the break?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: We have a break at noon.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: What time do we finish today?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: We finish at five, but we have overtime.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Why do we work overtime tonight?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.line: The deadline is Friday. Check the schedule.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.line: Okay. I understand the schedule now.

multipleChoice.prompt: Your work period for the day is called a ___.
multipleChoice.explanation: A shift is a work period.

multipleChoice.prompt: You rest and eat during a ___.
multipleChoice.explanation: A break is a rest time.

multipleChoice.prompt: The last day to finish the job is the ___.
multipleChoice.explanation: A deadline is the final day.

multipleChoice.prompt: You arrive after the start time. You are ___.
Extra work after normal hours is called ___.

fillBlank.prompt: My ___ starts at seven in the morning.
fillBlank.explanation: A shift is a work period.

fillBlank.prompt: Please do not be ___ for work.
fillBlank.explanation: Late means after the start time.

fillBlank.prompt: We must ___ the job before Friday.

matching.instruction: Match the word to its meaning.
Match the word to its meaning.
